mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers immediate positive repricing of equity assets over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, producing negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or loses significance when inflation exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation combined with restrictive federal funds rates compresses real yields and corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns dominate when both proxies are high, or the interaction effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the labor force and aggregate income, supporting higher corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in >60% of periods, or the predictive correlation drops below 0.05 over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms sustained economic expansion, which increases equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in >50% of triggered periods, or the threshold effect disappears when GDP growth is driven solely by inventory accumulation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a synchronized economic upturn, triggering immediate positive equity repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to predict positive returns when industrial production rises while unemployment also rises, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.15) in out-of-sample tests.
